itineraryStops.description: 從首爾兩個中央集合點擇一出發：**弘益大學站8號出口**或**明洞站2號或4號出口**。9小時行程設計使全團在人潮高峰前清空華城，留足時間進行南門市場＋星空圖書館＋光明洞窟的下午三連。**南下水原約1小時**，走京釜高速公路。

走京釜高速公路，南下水原約1小時

itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: An 09:30 Suwon arrival puts the group at Hwaseong Fortress with 60-90 min before midday crowds peak around 11:00. The day's clockwork — fortress → palace → market lunch → library → cave — minimizes coach repositioning and ends with the cave's reliable indoor finale.

smartNotes.tip: Confirm exact pickup time and meeting point at booking. Bring water, sunscreen, and comfortable shoes for fortress wall walking — and a light jacket for the cave (12°C year-round).

itineraryStops.name: 水原華城
itineraryStops.description: **水原華城（수원화성）——1997年UNESCO世界文化遺產**——是韓國唯一同時保存有原始10冊施工記錄（《華城城役儀軌》，2007年另列入UNESCO世界記憶遺產）的城牆要塞城市。由正祖大王命令於**1794至1796年，僅2年9個月**建成，設計者丁若鏞融合韓式、中式及西方城防科學，並發明거중기滑輪組吊車。城牆全周長**5.74公里**，設41處軍事設施。今日導覽步行長安門至華西門間最密集的約1.2公里段。**華城行宮**——韓國現存最大行宮（原576間），洛南軒是現場唯一保存自1796年的原建築。**換崗儀式**週二至日上午11:00在新豐樓前舉行（免費，約15分鐘）。韓劇《大長今》（2003年）取景地。**門票含括在行程費用內**。
highlights: **1997年UNESCO世界文化遺產**——韓國唯一保存原施工記錄的城牆要塞
1794至1796年正祖大王建造，丁若鏞設計（韓式＋中式＋西方城防）
全周長5.74公里，41處軍事設施；導覽步行最密集的1.2公里段

itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: The route's UNESCO anchor — Korea's only remaining walled fortress city, built 1794-1796 under King Jeongjo. The Janganmun → Hwaseomun walk gives the highest density of preserved military structures in a 60-min stop, sized for a 9-hour day with 5 substantive stops. The full 5.74 km loop would consume 2.5+ hours and cannibalize the day's other heritage and modern stops. For this 5-stop itinerary, Hwaseong serves as the **morning anchor** — the 5.74 km perimeter is too long to circumnavigate in a single visit, so the day-trip format focuses on the Janganmun → Seojangdae western section (~25-30 min walking, highest preservation density). The 1801 **Hwaseong Seongyeok Uigwe** construction record — UNESCO Memory of the World since 2007 — documented every stone, beam, and laborer's wage, enabling faithful 1975-onwards reconstruction after Korean War damage. Jeong Yak-yong's **Geojunggi (거중기) pulley-and-block crane** is the world's first documented use of Western mechanical-advantage engineering on a Joseon-era construction site, predating Korea's industrial mechanization by ~120 years.

visitBasics.hours: Wall path 24 hours; recommended 09:00-18:00 summer / 09:00-17:00 winter
visitBasics.admission: Adults 1,000 / Youth 700 / Children 500 KRW (FREE: children ≤6, seniors 65+, hanbok-wearers, last Wed of month)
visitBasics.walking: Moderate — wall has steep sections; stairs at gates

smartNotes.photo: **Hwahongmun (north sluice gate)** with 7 arches over Suwoncheon Stream — the iconic shot. **Janganmun (largest existing royal gate in Korea)** at golden hour. **Seojangdae (west command post)** sunset over Paldalsan Mountain.
smartNotes.facilities: Visitor Information Centers at all main gates; English-Korean signage throughout
smartNotes.tip: Hanbok rental at Haenggung Square gives FREE fortress + palace admission. The Janganmun → Hwaseomun section gives the highest per-meter heritage density.

itineraryStops.name: 華城行宮
itineraryStops.description: **華城行宮（화성행궁）——韓國現存最大行宮（離宮）**，1789至1796年與華城城牆同步建造，供正祖大王13次前往祭拜被悶死的父親思悼世子時留宿。1795年皇室大移動（6,000名隨從＋1,400匹馬）記載於UNESCO世界記憶遺產。日據時代大部分拆毀；1996年起依儀軌復原，洛南軒是唯一原1796年真實建築，其他均為復原重建物。韓劇《大長今》取景地。**門票含括在行程費用內**。
highlights: **韓國現存最大行宮（原576間）**
正祖大王13次朝聖，1795年皇室大移動（UNESCO世界記憶遺產）
**洛南軒＝唯一原1796年建築**（其他均為依儀軌復原的重建物）
**換崗儀式週二至日上午11:00，新豐樓前（免費，約15分鐘）**
**門票含括在行程費用內**
timeUsed: 5 min walk from Hwaseong Fortress (Hwaseomun area)
Royal Guard Changing Ceremony at 11:00 (Tue-Sun) — ~15 min

Optional Royal Guard Changing Ceremony at Sinpungnu Gate (Tue-Sun 11:00 AM, ~15 min, FREE — weather permitting)
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: The temporary royal residence is INSIDE the same UNESCO site as Hwaseong Fortress, 5 min walk apart. **Naknamheon = the only authentic original structure** (others rebuilt post-1996). Time arrival for the 11:00 AM Royal Guard Changing Ceremony — FREE, 15 min, one of the catalog's strongest free-experience hooks. Haenggung occupies the **historical and emotional center** of the Hwaseong project — the fortress walls were built around it (1794-1796) precisely to defend King Jeongjo's filial-piety pilgrimages to his father's tomb. Visiting Haenggung after the wall stretch converts an abstract military-architecture lesson into lived royal-Joseon spatial experience: the same **Bongsudang throne hall** also hosted the lavish 60th-birthday banquet for Jeongjo's mother Lady Hyegyeong in 1795 (depicted in the surviving Uigwe Banchado court-painting scrolls). The site has appeared in numerous K-dramas including Jewel in the Palace (2003), Sungkyunkwan Scandal (2010), and Moonlight Drawn by Clouds (2016). A **Combined Fortress + Haenggung ticket** at 3,500 KRW (vs 2,500 KRW separately) makes Haenggung a high-value addition to the day's anchor sequence.

visitBasics.admission: Adult 1,500 / Teen 1,000 / Child 700 KRW. Combined Fortress + Haenggung 3,500 KRW. FREE for hanbok-wearers, seniors 65+
visitBasics.walking: Easy — palace grounds mostly flat with some steps between courtyards

convenience.restroom: At main entrance, near Bongsudang, at Haenggung Square
convenience.parking: Hwaseong Haenggung public parking; Haenggung Square paid lots

smartNotes.photo: **Bongsudang Main Hall** + **Naknamheon (the only original 1796 structure)** + **Royal Guard Changing Ceremony at Sinpungnu (Tue-Sun 11:00 AM)**.
smartNotes.facilities: Visitor Information Center, hanbok rental at Haenggung Square (15,000-20,000 KRW for 4 hr), Suwon Tongdak restaurants 5 min walk south.
smartNotes.tip: **Time arrival for 11:00 AM Royal Guard Changing Ceremony Tue-Sun (FREE)**. **Naknamheon is THE single most historically important spot** — the only structure not rebuilt, surviving Japanese colonial demolition. Skip Mondays — Royal Guard Changing Ceremony performed Tue-Sun only.

itineraryStops.name: 水原南門市場——午餐＋街頭美食
itineraryStops.description: **水原南門市場（수원남문시장）**——緊鄰**八達門（팔달문，華城南城門）**南側的歷史商業市場，名稱直譯為「南門市場」；起源追溯至**18世紀末**正祖建造華城（1794至1796年）後，商人在南城門外設攤。市場**持續運營約230年**，由**9個分市場網絡**組成，逾1,200家店鋪橫跨10公頃步行巷道。招牌景點：**水原통닭갈비街**——水原獨有的통닭（整雞烤）＋왕갈비（王式大肋骨燒烤）街，50年以上家族傳承的創始餐廳群。街頭美食：순대、떡볶이、호떡、빈대떡、魚板（每道₩3,000至₩7,000）。韓劇**《請回答1988》（2015年）**取景巷道。建議攜帶現金（₩20,000以上）。
highlights: **約230年傳統市場**——起源於正祖華城城市（1796年）
韓國中小企業廳指定「文化觀光市場」
9個分市場＋逾1,200家店鋪，橫跨10公頃步行巷道

itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Lunch at Suwon's most famous streetfood district — leveraging proximity (5 min walk from Hwaseong Haenggung) AND the Suwon Tongdak Galbi specialty (regional cuisine you cannot get in Seoul). Picked over Haenggung Square cafes (newer, less authentic) because Nammun Market provides the Joseon-era → working-class market continuity that elevates the day's heritage narrative. **CASH WARNING** built into pre-booking communication. Located **immediately south of Paldalmun** (the only fortress gate within central Suwon), Nammun Market connects the UNESCO walled district to its commercial-living context — the market literally translates as 'south-gate market.' The 9-sub-market network (Paldalmun + Yeongdong + Jidong + Motgol + Mi-charm + Mun-Ye + Nammun Rodeo + Yeongdong Suwon + Tongdak Galbi Street) is officially designated a **Korea SBA 'Cultural Tourism Market' (문화관광형시장)** — one of ~25 markets nationwide receiving government tourism investment. Best visited around **lunch (12:00-14:00) or dinner (17:00-20:00)** to catch the Tongdak Galbi cluster + streetfood at peak operation.

visitBasics.closed: Open year-round (individual stalls may close Sundays)
visitBasics.admission: Free entry; pay-as-you-go (cash recommended for streetfood; cards at sit-down restaurants)
visitBasics.walking: Easy — flat pedestrian alleys; cobbled in places

smartNotes.photo: **Paldalmun (south gate of Hwaseong) framing the market entrance** — the iconic shot. **Suwon Tongdak Galbi Street** at evening illumination. **Reply 1988 filming alleys** — small 1980s-style streets at the eastern end. Hanbok-wearers browsing the market.
smartNotes.facilities: Tourist Information Center at Paldalmun Plaza, ATMs, cash exchange shops, 24-hour convenience stores. Hwaseong Fortress access via Paldalmun directly.
smartNotes.tip: **BRING CASH 20,000+ KRW** — many streetfood stalls don't accept cards. **Lunch recommendation: Suwon Tongdak (whole roast chicken) + makgeolli** — local cultural pairing, ~12,000-18,000 KRW per person at Tongdak Street. Pair with Hwaseong Fortress: Paldalmun south gate is the natural transition between fortress wall and market lunch.

itineraryStops.name: 星空圖書館水原
itineraryStops.description: **星空圖書館水原（별마당 도서관 수원）**——位於**신世界星空水原購物中心（2024年1月26日開幕）**，約33萬4,000平方公尺，韓國第二大室內購物中心。由**BCHO Partners**設計，**22公尺高書架**是韓國最高的圓柱形書架建築之一。圖書館橫跨4層（一至四樓挑空中庭），館藏約7萬冊。**免費開放**，每日10:00至22:00。特色：黑膠唱片聆聽區、月度輪換當代藝術展、春季（3至4月）千朵粉色LED花瓣中庭裝置。地下一樓**Eatopia美食廣場**（30多家餐廳）。

itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: The 'modern Korea' counterweight after fortress + palace + market lunch. Shinsegae's flagship 2nd-generation Starfield mall opened January 26, 2024; the four-floor library with 22 m bookshelves is the architectural highlight. **All-indoor reset stop** — buffers afternoon weather risk and provides genuine multi-generational appeal before Gwangmyeong Cave's industrial-heritage finale. Starfield Library is the **single most Instagram-photographed indoor space in Greater Seoul outside central Seoul** (per Korean travel-media surveys 2024-25) — the 22m bookshelves (vs Seoul COEX's 13m) span mall floors 4F-7F as a connected atrium void. **FREE admission, no membership, photos permitted, mall-wide free entry** — a no-friction photo stop with optional cafe/dining adjacency (Eatopia food hall on B1F with 30+ Korean and international restaurants). From Suwon Station: free Starfield shuttle bus (every 10-15 min from Suwon Station Exit 4) or Subway Line 1 → Hwaseo Station Exit 1/4 (8-min walk). Mall hours **10:00-22:00 daily**, library accessible during all mall hours.

visitBasics.hours: Mall 10:00-22:00; library accessible during all mall hours
visitBasics.closed: 1-2 monthly closure days (typically Mondays — verify online)
visitBasics.admission: Library FREE; mall-wide free entry; Aquafield separate ~25,000-35,000 KRW
visitBasics.walking: Easy — escalators and elevators between all floors; wheelchair/stroller accessible

convenience.restroom: Multiple restrooms on every floor; family-friendly stations
convenience.parking: Underground ~5,000 spaces; first 3 hours FREE with mall purchase 30,000 KRW+; coach lot for groups

smartNotes.photo: **Library central atrium with 22-meter bookshelves** from 4F looking down. Vinyl Record Lounge for retro aesthetic. Cherry blossom installation in atrium (March-April). Christmas tree (December).
smartNotes.facilities: Eatopia food hall B1F, Toy Kingdom 4F, Aquafield water park B1-2F, T-Joy + CGV + MEGABOX cinemas, free WiFi, multilingual customer service, tax-refund counters.
smartNotes.tip: **Library is FREE** — exceptional value attraction. **22-meter bookshelves = Korea's tallest** (mention to guests for impact). **Cherry blossom installation March-April** is the seasonal must-see. Avoid Saturday afternoons (peak crowds). Verify Starfield monthly closure dates online.

itineraryStops.name: 光明洞窟
itineraryStops.description: **光明洞窟（광명동굴）**——原為**1912年日據時代開發的金、銀、銅、鋅礦**，運營60年至1972年。在開採期間，礦山產出52公斤黃金、6,070公斤白銀、1,247噸銅及3,247噸鋅。停產後，廢棄隧道被用作**새우젓（蝦醬）發酵儲存設施**約40年——洞窟全年恆溫12°C及高濕度，是韓國醃製海鮮陳化的理想環境。**光明市2011年以43億韓圜收購洞窟**，並於同年8月重新開放，成為**韓國第一座由政府擁有的洞窟觀光景點**。獲得**2014年韓國旅遊之星大獎**（韓國旅遊的奧斯卡）。公眾可進入的**路線約2公里**（洞窟總長7.8公里）。**主題展區**：**洞窟水族館**（韓國最深的室內淡水魚水族館）；**酒窖**（韓國最大地下酒窖，儲藏200種以上韓國及進口葡萄酒，葡萄酒品酒₩5,000 / 3杯）；**LED藝術隧道**（200公尺隧道，春季賞櫻、秋季楓葉、冬季雪景主題季節性燈光）；**龍丸（용마루）龍雕**（41公尺長玻璃纖維龍——韓國任何洞窟中最高的室內雕塑，最多人社交媒體分享的打卡點）；**熒光壁畫**（僅在紫外線 / 黑燈下可見的天然熒光顏料壁畫）；**音樂廳**（200席聲學最佳化表演場地）。韓劇**《阿爾罕布拉宮的回憶》（2018年）**取景地。年訪客約**150萬人次**（2024年數據）。**週一閉館**，週二至日09:00至18:00（最後入場17:00）。成人₩7,000 / 青少年₩3,500 / 兒童₩2,000。主要路線可輪椅通行。**門票含括在行程費用內**。

itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: 光明洞窟是全日「現代改造歷史空間」的最強力收尾——日據時代金礦搖身一變為充滿LED裝置藝術的地下奇幻世界，提供在首爾周邊找不到第二個類似的視覺體驗。全程室內設計完美緩衝任何傍晚天氣風險。

visitBasics.admission: Adult 7,000 / Youth 3,500 / Child 2,000 KRW; FREE for children ≤6 and seniors 65+

smartNotes.photo: **LED Art Tunnel** seasonal-themed lighting (spring cherry blossom, autumn foliage, winter snow) — most photographed zone. **41-m Dragon (Yongmaru)** — viral social-media spot. **Fluorescent Mural** under UV light. **Wine Cave bottle walls** with Korean wine producer labels.
smartNotes.facilities: Cave restaurant (Korean-style buffet ~15,000 KRW), café with cave-aged wine list, gift shop, Wine Cave tasting room (5,000 KRW for 3 wines), Music Hall performance schedule.
smartNotes.tip: **12°C year-round — bring light jacket** even in summer. Perfect for **summer escape from Korean heatwaves**. **Skip Mondays** — closed. **Wine tasting** (5,000 KRW for 3 wines) is the most underrated experience — Korean wines from regional wineries you can't find in Seoul. Plan 1.5-2 hours minimum; 2.5 hours with wine tasting and Music Hall concert.

routeShapeIntro.title: 行程動線總覽
routeShapeIntro.subtitle: A triangular Gyeonggi loop south and southwest of Seoul: Suwon's UNESCO fortress city for the morning, Suwon's flagship modern mall for early afternoon, and Gwangmyeong's transformed gold mine for the late-afternoon finale. Three different kinds of public space — military, commercial, industrial — across 250 years, in a single coach loop.

items.label: 三層時間軸——韓國歷史的三種面貌
items.detail: 華城（18世紀朝鮮UNESCO城牆）→星空圖書館（2024年現代韓國）→光明洞窟（1912年日據金礦→2011年再生為藝術空間）——三個景點各自代表不同時代的韓國，也各自代表不同的視覺語言：石砌城牆、22公尺書架、地下LED隧道。

items.label: 光明洞窟是首爾周邊找不到第二個的獨特景觀
items.detail: 前殖民時代金礦改建為充滿龍雕、LED隧道、地下酒窖和水族館的地下世界，是韓國所有日歸行程中最不一樣的視覺體驗——完全找不到類似的替代景點。

items.label: 南門市場作為地理和文化的過渡節點
items.detail: 南門市場緊靠華城南城門，承接城牆歷史的下午文化收尾，同時作為地理過渡點（華城→往西的光明）。230年持續運營的歷史使其不只是午餐停留，而是城市文化體驗的一部分。

items.label: Cooler than a single-format day
items.detail: Heritage tours can feel relentless. Library-and-mall tours can feel light. This itinerary deliberately shifts gears — UNESCO walls, market food, modern library, then an LED-lit underground theme park — so the day doesn't fall into a single rhythm.

routeLogicSections.title: How the walking load actually feels

items.label: Fortress wall: hardest section
items.detail: ≈1 km walked with steep gradients and stairs at gates. Comfortable shoes essential. Sweeping views reward the climb but it isn't wheelchair-friendly.

items.label: Gwangmyeong Cave: ≈180 stairs each way
items.detail: Stairs to the lower-level highlights (Wine Cave, Golden Waterfall, Aqua World) and the same back. No elevator. Visitors with mobility limits stay on the upper level — still ≈40 min of content there.

items.label: Haenggung, Nammun Market, Starfield: easy
items.detail: Palace and Starfield are flat and accessible. Nammun Market has crowds at lunch but no real walking load.

items.label: Summer heat: Gwangmyeong is the relief
items.detail: 12°C constant inside the cave makes it the refuge stop on hot days. The wall walk lengthens shade breaks; market lunch shifts indoors at Starfield.

items.label: Winter cold: layer for the cave
items.detail: 12°C is cool when outside is colder. Bring a light jacket inside Gwangmyeong even in summer; in winter, expect to keep your outdoor layer on.

items.label: Mondays are not available
items.detail: Gwangmyeong Cave closes Mondays for maintenance. Tour doesn't operate. Pick the Folk Village or Waujeongsa version for Monday travel.

practicalWeatherStatic._seasonalSummary: Suwon and Gwangmyeong track Seoul's weather closely (about 30 km south/southwest). Plan for hot, humid summers and cold, dry winters; the cave is a constant 12°C year-round, providing relief in summer and shelter in winter.

光明洞窟全年恆溫12°C，是夏日酷暑中最受歡迎的天然涼爽景點。城牆段日照強烈，建議戴帽。星空圖書館提供全程室內空調環境。

staticQuestions.question: 為什麼這個行程週一不開班？
staticQuestions.answer: 光明洞窟週一因維護作業閉館。由於洞窟是四大核心景點之一，週一不排班。若您想在週一造訪水原但跳過洞窟，可詢問業者是否有替代安排。

staticQuestions.question: 門票含括在行程費用內嗎？
staticQuestions.answer: 含括——水原華城、華城行宮及光明洞窟門票全部含括。星空圖書館對外免費開放。選做洞窟酒窖品酒（₩5,000 / 3杯）現場自費。

staticQuestions.question: 午餐含括嗎？
staticQuestions.answer: 不含。午餐通常在水原南門市場的街頭美食（₩3,000至₩7,000 / 道）或星空水原美食廣場（₩10,000至₩20,000）。導遊當日推薦最新選項。

staticQuestions.question: 光明洞窟有多少階樓梯？
staticQuestions.answer: 大約180階抵達下層精華展區（酒窖、黃金瀑布、水族館世界），回程同樣180階。無電梯。行動不便的旅客需認真考慮此段——下層展區是全日最具視覺衝擊力的部分，但樓梯無法迴避。

staticQuestions.question: 洞窟是溫的還是涼的？
staticQuestions.answer: 涼的。洞窟全年恆溫12°C（54°F）。夏季是清涼的避暑選項；冬季建議保留一件薄外套。夏季旅客通常攜帶輕薄夾克備用。

staticQuestions.question: 和水原＋民俗村行程有何不同？
staticQuestions.answer: 兩條路線均含華城、行宮及星空圖書館。本版本配合光明洞窟（地下LED藝術主題公園）和南門市場街頭美食。民俗村版本則配合韓國民俗村（260棟傳統建築、騎馬武藝表演）——偏向文化表演體驗。洞窟版本更適合喜歡獨特地下景觀及龍雕打卡的旅客。

staticQuestions.question: 這是初次造訪水原和光明的好選擇嗎？
staticQuestions.answer: 是的——對想在一日內體驗一個UNESCO遺址（華城）、一個朝鮮宮殿（行宮）、一個地下藝術景點（光明洞窟）和一個現代圖書館（星空）的旅客，是最強力的首選組合之一。

staticQuestions.question: 步行難度如何？
staticQuestions.answer: 全日活躍步行，含一段繁重階梯。華城城牆有坡度石階及不平整地面（約1公里）。光明洞窟下層精華展區需約180階下行＋180階返回。星空圖書館全程平坦室內。建議穿著有抓地力的舒適步行鞋。

staticQuestions.question: 親子家庭適合嗎？
staticQuestions.answer: 適合能應對城牆石階及洞窟180階的孩子。洞窟是孩子最愛——LED燈光、龍雕及水族館。城牆石階及長日程對年幼兒童較具挑戰性，建議約10歲以上。

staticQuestions.question: 年長旅客能否舒適地參加？
staticQuestions.answer: 建議能應對樓梯及封閉空間的活躍銀髮族。光明洞窟180階無電梯是最大限制——如有疑慮請提前告知導遊，可規劃略過下層，留在上層展區（仍有LED藝術隧道等精彩展示）。

staticQuestions.question: 建議最低年齡是多少？
staticQuestions.answer: 建議約10歲以上。城牆有不平整地面，洞窟有180階，全日行程約9小時。較年幼的孩子可能在後半段體力不支，但對洞窟龍雕等視覺刺激通常反應積極。

itineraryStops.name: Suwon Hwaseong Fortress
itineraryStops.description: Guided walk along a section of the 5.7 km UNESCO-listed fortress wall, built 1794–1796 under King Jeongjo as a planned new capital. Highlights typically include Janganmun (the largest gate), Hwaseomun, beacon mounds, and bastions, with sweeping city views from the wall.
highlights: UNESCO since 1997
Major fortress gates
Beacon mounds and bastions
Sweeping city views
timeUsed: Main attraction exploration with photo time
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: The route's UNESCO anchor — Korea's only remaining walled fortress city, built 1794-1796 under King Jeongjo. The walked section between Janganmun and Hwaseomun gives the highest density of named features per minute: largest gate, beacon mound, bastions, command posts. Sweeping city views from the wall reward the climb.

visitBasics.admission: Fortress wall: 1,000 KRW adult / 700 KRW youth / 500 KRW child (free for children ≤6, seniors 65+, hanbok wearers, last Wed of month)
visitBasics.walking: Moderate to challenging — wall has steep sections, stairs at gates and command posts; some sections wheelchair-difficult

convenience.restroom: Available at major gates and visitor centers

itineraryStops.name: Hwaseong Haenggung Palace
itineraryStops.description: The temporary royal residence inside the fortress, used by King Jeongjo when he visited his father's tomb. The largest haenggung (detached palace) in Korea. Live performances — royal guard ceremonies, martial arts demos — run on most days.

Martial-arts demonstrations
timeUsed: Main attraction exploration with photo time
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Pairs naturally with the wall — the same UNESCO site, 5 minutes apart. Korea's largest haenggung (detached palace), restored from 19th-century fire damage. Live royal-guard ceremonies and martial-arts demos add audio/visual variety after the quiet wall walk; performance times posted at the entrance.

itineraryStops.name: Suwon Nammun Market (lunch & street food)
itineraryStops.description: Free time at Suwon's largest traditional market — a 220-year-old market organized by King Jeongjo during Hwaseong's construction in the late 1700s, the only Korean market created by royal command. Located at Paldalmun (the fortress south gate), the market is famous for Suwon-style galbi, fried chicken (tongdak alley, ≈400 m of stalls), traditional sweets, and dozens of street-food kiosks. Nine sub-markets coexist under the Nammun umbrella; the guide recommends a few favorites if you want a sit-down lunch.
highlights: 220-year history (King Jeongjo's market, only royal-organized market in Korea)
Suwon-style galbi (signature dish from cattle-market era)
Tongdak (whole fried chicken) alley — ≈400 m of stalls
Coalition of 9 sub-markets, the largest in Gyeonggi

itineraryStops.name: Starfield Library, Suwon
itineraryStops.description: Inside Starfield Suwon — Shinsegae's flagship 'second-generation' Starfield mall opened January 2024 — the library spans four floors with bookshelves up to 22 meters tall, a vinyl-record listening area, and curated reading lounges. Bigger and quieter than the original Starfield Library at COEX in Seoul. Free time for photos, browsing, and coffee.

Shinsegae's flagship 2nd-generation Starfield mall opened January 2024; four-floor library with 22 m bookshelves and vinyl-listening seats is the day's contemporary photo. Bigger and quieter than the original COEX library. Free entry, escalator-accessible.

itineraryStops.name: Gwangmyeong Cave
itineraryStops.description: A 30-minute drive to Gwangmyeong, where the former Siheung gold mine — opened by Japanese colonial authorities in 1912 and worked under forced labor — has been reborn as Korea's largest themed cave park. The mine produced gold, silver, copper, and zinc until closure in 1972, then served as a salted-shrimp storage facility for 40 years before Gwangmyeong city converted it into a tourist attraction in 2011. Its 7.8 km of underground tunnels now host LED-lit tableaux, an underground winery (Wine Cave, 170+ Korean wines), an Aqua World aquarium, the Golden Waterfall, the Golden Road of wishes, and Korea's largest dragon sculpture — a 41 m, 800 kg sculpture named 'Lord of the Cave' built by Weta Workshop (the New Zealand prop studio behind Lord of the Rings). Year-round 12°C inside — an air-conditioned experience in summer, a sheltered one in winter.

itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: The day's most distinctive contrast: a Japanese-colonial-era gold and silver mine reborn as Korea's largest themed cave park. 7.8 km of underground tunnels with LED tableaux, an underground winery, an Aqua World aquarium, the Golden Waterfall, and Korea's largest dragon sculpture (41 m). The constant 12°C inside makes it a relief stop in summer and a sheltered one in winter.

routeShapeIntro.title: How this day flows
routeShapeIntro.subtitle: A triangular Gyeonggi loop south and southwest of Seoul: Suwon's UNESCO fortress city for the morning, Suwon's flagship modern mall for early afternoon, and Gwangmyeong's transformed gold mine for the late-afternoon finale. Three different kinds of public space — military, commercial, industrial — across 250 years, in a single coach loop.

bestFor: Active travelers comfortable with full days
Travelers who want UNESCO World Heritage stops
lessIdealFor: Travelers needing a tight same-day flight buffer

routeLogicSections.title: Why this route is built this way

items.label: More variety than the standard Suwon tour
items.detail: Most Suwon day tours combine the fortress, palace, and Starfield Library — three stops within ten minutes of each other. This version adds Gwangmyeong Cave, a completely different landscape (underground, art-themed, year-round 12°C) that you can't easily reach from Suwon by public transit.

items.label: Indoor-friendly fallback for any weather
items.detail: Two of the four major stops — Starfield Library and Gwangmyeong Cave — are fully indoors and climate-controlled. The fortress walks are short enough to handle rain or summer heat. A reliable choice when the weather is dramatic.

items.label: Free admission, paid context
items.detail: Starfield Library is free to enter on your own — but it's much more rewarding with a guide who can explain why a mall built it (Shinsegae's MZ-generation strategy), how it differs from the COEX original, and where the best photo angles are without disturbing readers.

items.label: Cooler than a single-format day
items.detail: Heritage tours can feel relentless. Library-and-mall tours can feel light. This itinerary deliberately shifts gears — UNESCO walls, market food, modern library, then an LED-lit underground theme park — so the day doesn't fall into a single rhythm.

practicalAccordionItems.title: What's included
practicalAccordionItems.content: Round-trip coach transport from Seoul, English-speaking tour guide, Suwon Hwaseong Fortress admission, Hwaseong Haenggung Palace admission, Gwangmyeong Cave admission. Not included: meals, drinks, optional cave-winery tasting, tips. Starfield Library entry is free.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Pickup & drop-off
practicalAccordionItems.content: Hongik University Station (Exit 8) at ≈08:00 or Myeongdong Station (Exit 2 or 4) at ≈08:30. Arrive 10 minutes early. There is no hotel pickup; meeting points only. Latecomers cannot be accommodated and are non-refundable.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Walking & terrain
practicalAccordionItems.content: Moderate. The fortress wall walk involves uphill stone steps. Gwangmyeong Cave has approximately 180 steps down to the lower levels and back up — there is no elevator. Comfortable closed-toe walking shoes are essential. Not recommended if you have significant mobility limitations.

practicalAccordionItems.title: What to wear & bring
practicalAccordionItems.content: Comfortable walking shoes (sneakers or hiking shoes), a light jacket — Gwangmyeong Cave is 12°C year-round, so even in summer it's chilly. In winter, bring a warm coat for the fortress wall and a lighter layer for the cave. Camera, water, cash for lunch and street food (₩10,000–₩20,000 budget).

practicalAccordionItems.title: Cancellation
practicalAccordionItems.content: Free cancellation up to 24 hours before the tour starts. Same-day cancellations and no-shows are non-refundable. Tour does not operate on Mondays (Gwangmyeong Cave closed).

practicalAccordionItems.title: Children & accessibility
practicalAccordionItems.content: Children typically enjoy this tour — the cave's lights, dragon sculpture, and Aqua World are popular with families. The fortress walk has stairs and uneven surfaces. The cave's 180+ stairs make it unsuitable for guests with mobility issues, strollers, or fear of confined spaces.

practicalWeatherStatic._seasonalSummary: Suwon and Gwangmyeong track Seoul's weather closely (about 30 km south/southwest). Plan for hot, humid summers and cold, dry winters; the cave is a constant 12°C year-round, providing relief in summer and shelter in winter.

staticQuestions.question: Why isn't the tour available on Mondays?
staticQuestions.answer: Gwangmyeong Cave is closed on Mondays for maintenance. Since the cave is one of the four core stops, the tour doesn't operate on Mondays. If you want to visit on a Monday, consider the Suwon-only itineraries (Hwaseong + Folk Village or Hwaseong + Waujeongsa).

staticQuestions.question: Are admissions included?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes — Suwon Hwaseong Fortress, Hwaseong Haenggung Palace, and Gwangmyeong Cave admissions are all included. Starfield Library is free for everyone. Optional cave-winery tasting and any folk-village extras are at your own cost.

staticQuestions.question: Is lunch included?
staticQuestions.answer: No. Lunch is typically eaten during free time at Suwon Nammun Market (street food, Suwon galbi) or Starfield Suwon's food hall. Budget ₩10,000–₩20,000 per person.

staticQuestions.question: How many stairs are in Gwangmyeong Cave?
staticQuestions.answer: Approximately 180 steps down to the lower-level highlights (Wine Cave, Golden Waterfall, Aqua World) and the same back up. There is no elevator. Visitors with mobility issues or fear of confined spaces typically stay on the upper level.

staticQuestions.question: Is the cave warm or cold?
staticQuestions.answer: Cool. The cave is a constant 12°C (54°F) year-round. In summer that's a relief; in winter, you'll want to keep a layer on. Summer visitors often bring a light jacket just for this stop.

staticQuestions.question: How is this different from the Suwon + Folk Village tour?
staticQuestions.answer: Both visit the fortress, palace, and Starfield Library. This version pairs them with Gwangmyeong Cave (an underground LED-lit theme park) and Suwon Nammun Market. The Folk Village version drops the cave and adds the Korean Folk Village in Yongin (a 270-house open-air heritage park). Choose this one for variety and indoor weather; the Folk Village version for deeper Joseon-era heritage.

staticQuestions.question: Is this a good choice for first-time visitors to Suwon & Gwangmyeong?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes — strong first-time pick for travelers who want one UNESCO site (Hwaseong Fortress), one Joseon palace (Haenggung), one underground themed attraction (Gwangmyeong Cave), and one contemporary photo space (Starfield Library) in a single day. The format gives four very different cultural angles. Note: the tour doesn't operate on Mondays (Gwangmyeong Cave maintenance day).

staticQuestions.question: Is the walking difficult?
staticQuestions.answer: Active walking day with one heavy stair section. Hwaseong wall has steep sections and stairs (≈1 km walked). Gwangmyeong Cave's lower-level highlights require ≈180 stairs down and the same back up — no elevator. Haenggung Palace is mostly flat with some courtyard steps. Starfield Library is fully escalator/elevator-accessible. Total walking ≈4 km. Comfortable shoes essential, especially for the cave stairs.

staticQuestions.question: Is this tour suitable for families?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes for families whose kids can handle the wall walk and Gwangmyeong's stairs. The cave is a kid favorite — LED-lit tableaux, Aqua World aquarium, the 41 m dragon sculpture, the underground winery (parents only) and Golden Waterfall. Strollers don't work on the wall or in the cave's stair sections. Suwon Nammun Market is a hit for street-food-curious teens. Year-round 12°C inside the cave makes it a relief stop.

staticQuestions.question: Can older adults join comfortably?
staticQuestions.answer: Recommended only for active seniors comfortable with stairs and confined spaces. The 180 stairs at Gwangmyeong's lower levels have no elevator alternative — the upper level (≈40 min of content) is accessible if stairs aren't possible. The fortress wall has steep sections; easier flat segments are walkable. Haenggung and Starfield are fully accessible. Bring a light layer for the cave even in summer.

staticQuestions.question: What's the recommended minimum age?
staticQuestions.answer: Best fit is around age 10 and up. The fortress wall has uneven surfaces, the cave has 180 stairs each way, and the day is long (9 hours). Younger kids can manage if comfortable on stairs and with confined spaces (the cave's lower tunnels are narrow). Kids often love the cave's underground attractions — dragon sculpture, Aqua World, Wine Cave (visual only). The Suwon street-food market works for all ages.
